content.thinking: Now let me check the planning-with-files Codex documentation to understand what it offers for Codex compatibility.

content.content:
1	# Codex Setup
2	
3	Using planning-with-files with [OpenAI Codex](https://developers.openai.com/codex/).
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Overview
8	
9	Codex discovers skills from `.codex/skills/` and hooks from `.codex/hooks.json` or `~/.codex/hooks.json`.
10	
11	This integration includes both:
12	
13	- `.codex/skills/planning-with-files/` for the skill itself
14	- `.codex/hooks.json` plus `.codex/hooks/` for lifecycle automation
15	
16	The hook behavior reuses the same mature shell scripts as the Cursor integration, with a thin Codex adapter layer for the differences in hook protocol.
17	
18	> **Important:** Codex hooks require `codex_hooks = true` in `~/.codex/config.toml`.
19	
20	---
21	
22	## Installation
23	
24	### Method 1: Workspace Installation (Recommended)
25	
26	Share the skill and hooks with your whole team by committing `.codex/` to your repository:
27	
28	```bash
29	# In your project repository
30	git clone https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files.git /tmp/planning-with-files
31	
32	# Copy the Codex integration to your repo
33	cp -r /tmp/planning-with-files/.codex .
34	
35	# Commit to share with team
36	git add .codex/
37	git commit -m "Add planning-with-files skill for Codex"
38	git push
39	
40	# Clean up
41	rm -rf /tmp/planning-with-files
42	```
43	
44	### Method 2: Personal Installation
45	
46	Install just for yourself:
47	
48	```bash
49	# Clone the repo
50	git clone https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files.git /tmp/planning-with-files
51	
52	# Copy the skill
53	mkdir -p ~/.codex/skills
54	cp -r /tmp/planning-with-files/.codex/skills/planning-with-files ~/.codex/skills/
55	
56	# Copy the hook scripts
57	mkdir -p ~/.codex/hooks
58	cp -r /tmp/planning-with-files/.codex/hooks/* ~/.codex/hooks/
59	
60	# Copy hooks.json
61	# If you already have ~/.codex/hooks.json, merge the planning-with-files entries manually
62	cp /tmp/planning-with-files/.codex/hooks.json ~/.codex/hooks.json
63	
64	# Clean up
65	rm -rf /tmp/planning-with-files
66	```
67	
68	> **Note:** If you already have a `~/.codex/hooks.json`, do not overwrite it blindly. Merge the `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `PreToolUse`, `PostToolUse`, and `Stop` entries into your existing file.
69	
70	### Enable Hooks in `config.toml`
71	
72	Ensure your `~/.codex/config.toml` contains:
73	
74	```toml
75	[features]
76	codex_hooks = true
77	```
78	
79	If you already have a `[features]` section, add `codex_hooks = true` under it instead of creating a duplicate section.
80	
81	### Verification
82	
83	```bash
84	codex --version
85	codex features list | rg '^codex_hooks\s'
86	ls -la ~/.codex/skills/planning-with-files/SKILL.md
87	ls -la ~/.codex/hooks.json ~/.codex/hooks/
88	```
89	
90	If `codex_hooks` does not appear in `codex features list`, upgrade Codex before troubleshooting the skill.
91	
92	---
93	
94	## How It Works
95	
96	### Hooks
97	
98	Codex reads hooks from:
99	
100	1. `.codex/hooks.json` in your project root
101	2. `~/.codex/hooks.json` for your global install
102	
103	This integration includes all five Codex lifecycle hooks:
104	
105	| Hook | What It Does |
106	|------|--------------|
107	| **SessionStart** | Runs `session-catchup.py`, then injects active plan context |
108	| **UserPromptSubmit** | Re-injects plan and recent progress on every user message |
109	| **PreToolUse** | Re-reads the first 30 lines of `task_plan.md` before Bash |
110	| **PostToolUse** | Reminds the agent to update `progress.md` after Bash activity |
111	| **Stop** | Blocks once when phases are incomplete, then falls back to a follow-up reminder |
112	
113	### The Three Files
114	
115	Once activated, the skill creates and maintains:
116	
117	| File | Purpose | Location |
118	|------|---------|----------|
119	| `task_plan.md` | Phases, progress, decisions | Your project root |
120	| `findings.md` | Research, discoveries | Your project root |
121	| `progress.md` | Session log, test results | Your project root |
122	
123	---
124	
125	## Team Workflow
126	
127	### Workspace Installation
128	
129	With workspace installation (`.codex/` committed to your repo):
130	
131	- Everyone on the team gets the same skill and hooks
132	- The Codex setup is version controlled with the project
133	- Updates ship through normal git review
134	
135	### Personal Installation
136	
137	With personal installation (`~/.codex/`):
138	
139	- You can use the skill across all projects
140	- You keep your setup even if you change repositories
141	- Existing global hooks may need manual merging
142	
143	---
144	
145	## Troubleshooting
146	
147	### Hooks Not Running?
148	
149	1. Check that `codex_hooks = true` is present in `~/.codex/config.toml`
150	2. Verify `.codex/hooks.json` or `~/.codex/hooks.json` exists
151	3. Restart Codex after adding or changing hooks
152	4. Run `codex features list | rg '^codex_hooks\s'`
153	
154	### Already Using Other Global Hooks?
155	
156	That is fine, but do not overwrite your existing `~/.codex/hooks.json`. Merge the planning-with-files entries instead.
157	
158	### Seeing Duplicate Hook Messages?
159	
160	Avoid installing the same planning-with-files hooks in both places at once:
161	
162	- workspace `.codex/hooks.json`
163	- global `~/.codex/hooks.json`
164	
165	If you enable both, Codex may run both sets of hooks and duplicate the reminders.
166	
167	### Windows Support
168

file.content:
# Codex Setup

Using planning-with-files with [OpenAI Codex](https://developers.openai.com/codex/).

---

## Overview

Codex discovers skills from `.codex/skills/` and hooks from `.codex/hooks.json` or `~/.codex/hooks.json`.

This integration includes both:

- `.codex/skills/planning-with-files/` for the skill itself
- `.codex/hooks.json` plus `.codex/hooks/` for lifecycle automation

The hook behavior reuses the same mature shell scripts as the Cursor integration, with a thin Codex adapter layer for the differences in hook protocol.

> **Important:** Codex hooks require `codex_hooks = true` in `~/.codex/config.toml`.

---

## Installation

### Method 1: Workspace Installation (Recommended)

Share the skill and hooks with your whole team by committing `.codex/` to your repository:

```bash
# In your project repository
git clone https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files.git /tmp/planning-with-files

# Copy the Codex integration to your repo
cp -r /tmp/planning-with-files/.codex .

# Commit to share with team
git add .codex/
git commit -m "Add planning-with-files skill for Codex"
git push

# Clean up
rm -rf /tmp/planning-with-files
```

### Method 2: Personal Installation

Install just for yourself:

```bash
# Clone the repo
git clone https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files.git /tmp/planning-with-files

# Copy the skill
mkdir -p ~/.codex/skills
cp -r /tmp/planning-with-files/.codex/skills/planning-with-files ~/.codex/skills/

# Copy the hook scripts
mkdir -p ~/.codex/hooks
cp -r /tmp/planning-with-files/.codex/hooks/* ~/.codex/hooks/

# Copy hooks.json
# If you already have ~/.codex/hooks.json, merge the planning-with-files entries manually
cp /tmp/planning-with-files/.codex/hooks.json ~/.codex/hooks.json

# Clean up
rm -rf /tmp/planning-with-files
```

> **Note:** If you already have a `~/.codex/hooks.json`, do not overwrite it blindly. Merge the `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `PreToolUse`, `PostToolUse`, and `Stop` entries into your existing file.

### Enable Hooks in `config.toml`

Ensure your `~/.codex/config.toml` contains:

```toml
[features]
codex_hooks = true
```

If you already have a `[features]` section, add `codex_hooks = true` under it instead of creating a duplicate section.

### Verification

```bash
codex --version
codex features list | rg '^codex_hooks\s'
ls -la ~/.codex/skills/planning-with-files/SKILL.md
ls -la ~/.codex/hooks.json ~/.codex/hooks/
```

If `codex_hooks` does not appear in `codex features list`, upgrade Codex before troubleshooting the skill.

---

## How It Works

### Hooks

Codex reads hooks from:

1. `.codex/hooks.json` in your project root
2. `~/.codex/hooks.json` for your global install

This integration includes all five Codex lifecycle hooks:

| Hook | What It Does |
|------|--------------|
| **SessionStart** | Runs `session-catchup.py`, then injects active plan context |
| **UserPromptSubmit** | Re-injects plan and recent progress on every user message |
| **PreToolUse** | Re-reads the first 30 lines of `task_plan.md` before Bash |
| **PostToolUse** | Reminds the agent to update `progress.md` after Bash activity |
| **Stop** | Blocks once when phases are incomplete, then falls back to a follow-up reminder |

### The Three Files

Once activated, the skill creates and maintains:

| File | Purpose | Location |
|------|---------|----------|
| `task_plan.md` | Phases, progress, decisions | Your project root |
| `findings.md` | Research, discoveries | Your project root |
| `progress.md` | Session log, test results | Your project root |

---

## Team Workflow

### Workspace Installation

With workspace installation (`.codex/` committed to your repo):

- Everyone on the team gets the same skill and hooks
- The Codex setup is version controlled with the project
- Updates ship through normal git review

### Personal Installation

With personal installation (`~/.codex/`):

- You can use the skill across all projects
- You keep your setup even if you change repositories
- Existing global hooks may need manual merging

---

## Troubleshooting

### Hooks Not Running?

1. Check that `codex_hooks = true` is present in `~/.codex/config.toml`
2. Verify `.codex/hooks.json` or `~/.codex/hooks.json` exists
3. Restart Codex after adding or changing hooks
4. Run `codex features list | rg '^codex_hooks\s'`

### Already Using Other Global Hooks?

That is fine, but do not overwrite your existing `~/.codex/hooks.json`. Merge the planning-with-files entries instead.

### Seeing Duplicate Hook Messages?

Avoid installing the same planning-with-files hooks in both places at once:

- workspace `.codex/hooks.json`
- global `~/.codex/hooks.json`

If you enable both, Codex may run both sets of hooks and duplicate the reminders.

### Windows Support

OpenAI's current Codex hooks documentation says hooks are disabled on Windows. The skill files can still be installed there, but the hook automation is currently for macOS/Linux Codex environments.

---

## Learn More

- [Installation Guide](installation.md)
- [Quick Start](quickstart.md)
- [Workflow Diagram](workflow.md)

---

## Support

- **GitHub Issues:** https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files/issues
- **OpenAI Codex Hooks Docs:** https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks
- **OpenAI Codex Skills Docs:** https://developers.openai.com/codex/skills

1	# Codex App Compatibility: Worktree and Finishing Skill Adaptation
2	
3	Make superpowers skills work in the Codex App's sandboxed worktree environment without breaking existing Claude Code or Codex CLI behavior.
4	
5	**Ticket:** PRI-823
6	
7	## Motivation
8	
9	The Codex App runs agents inside git worktrees it manages — detached HEAD, located under `$CODEX_HOME/worktrees/`, with a Seatbelt sandbox that blocks `git checkout -b`, `git push`, and network access. Three superpowers skills assume unrestricted git access: `using-git-worktrees` creates manual worktrees with named branches, `finishing-a-development-branch` merges/pushes/PRs by branch name, and `subagent-driven-development` requires both.
10	
11	The Codex CLI (open source terminal tool) does NOT have this conflict — it has no built-in worktree management. Our manual worktree approach fills an isolation gap there. The problem is specifically with the Codex App.
12	
13	## Empirical Findings
14	
15	Tested in the Codex App on 2026-03-23:
16	
17	| Operation | workspace-write sandbox | Full access sandbox |
18	|---|---|---|
19	| `git add` | Works | Works |
20	| `git commit` | Works | Works |
21	| `git checkout -b` | **Blocked** (can't write `.git/refs/heads/`) | Works |
22	| `git push` | **Blocked** (network + `.git/refs/remotes/`) | Works |
23	| `gh pr create` | **Blocked** (network) | Works |
24	| `git status/diff/log` | Works | Works |
25	
26	Additional findings:
27	- `spawn_agent` subagents **share** the parent thread's filesystem (confirmed via marker file test)
28	- "Create branch" button appears in the App header regardless of which branch the worktree was started from
29	- The App's native finishing flow: Create branch → Commit modal → Commit and push / Commit and create PR
30	- `network_access = true` config is silently broken on macOS (issue #10390)
31	
32	## Design: Read-Only Environment Detection
33	
34	Three read-only git commands detect the environment without side effects:
35	
36	```bash
37	GIT_DIR=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
38	GIT_COMMON=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
39	BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
40	```
41	
42	Two signals derived:
43	
44	- **IN_LINKED_WORKTREE:** `GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON` — the agent is in a worktree created by something else (Codex App, Claude Code Agent tool, previous skill run, or the user)
45	- **ON_DETACHED_HEAD:** `BRANCH` is empty — no named branch exists
46	
47	Why `git-dir != git-common-dir` instead of checking `show-toplevel`:
48	- In a normal repo, both resolve to the same `.git` directory
49	- In a linked worktree, `git-dir` is `.git/worktrees/<name>` while `git-common-dir` is `.git`
50	- In a submodule, both are equal — avoiding a false positive that `show-toplevel` would produce
51	- Resolving via `cd && pwd -P` handles the relative-path problem (`git-common-dir` returns `.git` relative in normal repos but absolute in worktrees) and symlinks (macOS `/tmp` → `/private/tmp`)
52	
53	### Decision Matrix
54	
55	| Linked Worktree? | Detached HEAD? | Environment | Action |
56	|---|---|---|---|
57	| No | No | Claude Code / Codex CLI / normal git | Full skill behavior (unchanged) |
58	| Yes | Yes | Codex App worktree (workspace-write) | Skip worktree creation; handoff payload at finish |
59	| Yes | No | Codex App (Full access) or manual worktree | Skip worktree creation; full finishing flow |
60	| No | Yes | Unusual (manual detached HEAD) | Create worktree normally; warn at finish |
61	
62	## Changes
63	
64	### 1. `using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md` — Add Step 0 (~12 lines)
65	
66	New section between "Overview" and "Directory Selection Process":
67	
68	**Step 0: Check if Already in an Isolated Workspace**
69	
70	Run the detection commands. If `GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON`, skip worktree creation entirely. Instead:
71	1. Skip to "Run Project Setup" subsection under Creation Steps — `npm install` etc. is idempotent, worth running for safety
72	2. Then "Verify Clean Baseline" — run tests
73	3. Report with branch state:
74	   - On a branch: "Already in an isolated workspace at `<path>` on branch `<name>`. Tests passing. Ready to implement."
75	   - Detached HEAD: "Already in an isolated workspace at `<path>` (detached HEAD, externally managed). Tests passing. Note: branch creation needed at finish time. Ready to implement."
76	
77	If `GIT_DIR == GIT_COMMON`, proceed with the full worktree creation flow (unchanged).
78	
79	Safety verification (.gitignore check) is skipped when Step 0 fires — irrelevant for externally-created worktrees.
80	
81	Update the Integration section's "Called by" entries. Change the description on each from context-specific text to: "Ensures isolated workspace (creates one or verifies existing)". For example, the `subagent-driven-development` entry changes from "REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting" to "REQUIRED: Ensures isolated workspace (creates one or verifies existing)".
82	
83	**Sandbox fallback:** If `GIT_DIR == GIT_COMMON` and the skill proceeds to Creation Steps, but `git worktree add -b` fails with a permission error (e.g., Seatbelt sandbox denial), treat this as a late-detected restricted environment. Fall back to the Step 0 "already in workspace" behavior — skip creation, run setup and baseline tests in the current directory, report accordingly.
84	
85	After reporting in Step 0, STOP. Do not continue to Directory Selection or Creation Steps.
86	
87	**Everything else unchanged:** Directory Selection, Safety Verification, Creation Steps, Project Setup, Baseline Tests, Quick Reference, Common Mistakes, Red Flags.
88	
89	### 2. `finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md` — Add Step 1.5 + cleanup guard (~20 lines)
90	
91	**Step 1.5: Detect Environment** (after Step 1 "Verify Tests", before Step 2 "Determine Base Branch")
92	
93	Run the detection commands. Three paths:
94	
95	- **Path A** skips Steps 2 and 3 entirely (no base branch or options needed).
96	- **Paths B and C** proceed through Step 2 (Determine Base Branch) and Step 3 (Present Options) as normal.
97	
98	**Path A — Externally managed worktree + detached HEAD** (`GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON` AND `BRANCH` empty):
99	
100	First, ensure all work is staged and committed (`git add` + `git commit`). The Codex App's finishing controls operate on committed work.

file.content:
# Codex App Compatibility: Worktree and Finishing Skill Adaptation

Make superpowers skills work in the Codex App's sandboxed worktree environment without breaking existing Claude Code or Codex CLI behavior.

**Ticket:** PRI-823

## Motivation

The Codex App runs agents inside git worktrees it manages — detached HEAD, located under `$CODEX_HOME/worktrees/`, with a Seatbelt sandbox that blocks `git checkout -b`, `git push`, and network access. Three superpowers skills assume unrestricted git access: `using-git-worktrees` creates manual worktrees with named branches, `finishing-a-development-branch` merges/pushes/PRs by branch name, and `subagent-driven-development` requires both.

The Codex CLI (open source terminal tool) does NOT have this conflict — it has no built-in worktree management. Our manual worktree approach fills an isolation gap there. The problem is specifically with the Codex App.

## Empirical Findings

Tested in the Codex App on 2026-03-23:

| Operation | workspace-write sandbox | Full access sandbox |
|---|---|---|
| `git add` | Works | Works |
| `git commit` | Works | Works |
| `git checkout -b` | **Blocked** (can't write `.git/refs/heads/`) | Works |
| `git push` | **Blocked** (network + `.git/refs/remotes/`) | Works |
| `gh pr create` | **Blocked** (network) | Works |
| `git status/diff/log` | Works | Works |

Additional findings:
- `spawn_agent` subagents **share** the parent thread's filesystem (confirmed via marker file test)
- "Create branch" button appears in the App header regardless of which branch the worktree was started from
- The App's native finishing flow: Create branch → Commit modal → Commit and push / Commit and create PR
- `network_access = true` config is silently broken on macOS (issue #10390)

## Design: Read-Only Environment Detection

Three read-only git commands detect the environment without side effects:

```bash
GIT_DIR=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
GIT_COMMON=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
```

Two signals derived:

- **IN_LINKED_WORKTREE:** `GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON` — the agent is in a worktree created by something else (Codex App, Claude Code Agent tool, previous skill run, or the user)
- **ON_DETACHED_HEAD:** `BRANCH` is empty — no named branch exists

Why `git-dir != git-common-dir` instead of checking `show-toplevel`:
- In a normal repo, both resolve to the same `.git` directory
- In a linked worktree, `git-dir` is `.git/worktrees/<name>` while `git-common-dir` is `.git`
- In a submodule, both are equal — avoiding a false positive that `show-toplevel` would produce
- Resolving via `cd && pwd -P` handles the relative-path problem (`git-common-dir` returns `.git` relative in normal repos but absolute in worktrees) and symlinks (macOS `/tmp` → `/private/tmp`)

### Decision Matrix

| Linked Worktree? | Detached HEAD? | Environment | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| No | No | Claude Code / Codex CLI / normal git | Full skill behavior (unchanged) |
| Yes | Yes | Codex App worktree (workspace-write) | Skip worktree creation; handoff payload at finish |
| Yes | No | Codex App (Full access) or manual worktree | Skip worktree creation; full finishing flow |
| No | Yes | Unusual (manual detached HEAD) | Create worktree normally; warn at finish |

## Changes

### 1. `using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md` — Add Step 0 (~12 lines)

New section between "Overview" and "Directory Selection Process":

**Step 0: Check if Already in an Isolated Workspace**

Run the detection commands. If `GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON`, skip worktree creation entirely. Instead:
1. Skip to "Run Project Setup" subsection under Creation Steps — `npm install` etc. is idempotent, worth running for safety
2. Then "Verify Clean Baseline" — run tests
3. Report with branch state:
   - On a branch: "Already in an isolated workspace at `<path>` on branch `<name>`. Tests passing. Ready to implement."
   - Detached HEAD: "Already in an isolated workspace at `<path>` (detached HEAD, externally managed). Tests passing. Note: branch creation needed at finish time. Ready to implement."

If `GIT_DIR == GIT_COMMON`, proceed with the full worktree creation flow (unchanged).

Safety verification (.gitignore check) is skipped when Step 0 fires — irrelevant for externally-created worktrees.

Update the Integration section's "Called by" entries. Change the description on each from context-specific text to: "Ensures isolated workspace (creates one or verifies existing)". For example, the `subagent-driven-development` entry changes from "REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting" to "REQUIRED: Ensures isolated workspace (creates one or verifies existing)".

**Sandbox fallback:** If `GIT_DIR == GIT_COMMON` and the skill proceeds to Creation Steps, but `git worktree add -b` fails with a permission error (e.g., Seatbelt sandbox denial), treat this as a late-detected restricted environment. Fall back to the Step 0 "already in workspace" behavior — skip creation, run setup and baseline tests in the current directory, report accordingly.

After reporting in Step 0, STOP. Do not continue to Directory Selection or Creation Steps.

**Everything else unchanged:** Directory Selection, Safety Verification, Creation Steps, Project Setup, Baseline Tests, Quick Reference, Common Mistakes, Red Flags.

### 2. `finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md` — Add Step 1.5 + cleanup guard (~20 lines)

**Step 1.5: Detect Environment** (after Step 1 "Verify Tests", before Step 2 "Determine Base Branch")

Run the detection commands. Three paths:

- **Path A** skips Steps 2 and 3 entirely (no base branch or options needed).
- **Paths B and C** proceed through Step 2 (Determine Base Branch) and Step 3 (Present Options) as normal.

**Path A — Externally managed worktree + detached HEAD** (`GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON` AND `BRANCH` empty):

First, ensure all work is staged and committed (`git add` + `git commit`). The Codex App's finishing controls operate on committed work.

The answer is:
1. **Superpowers already has Codex compatibility built-in** - it has a `codex-tools.md` reference file that maps Claude Code tools to Codex equivalents
2. **planning-with-files** has both Claude Code and Codex support - it includes `.codex/` configuration for Codex
3. The Superpowers skills are designed to work across platforms (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode) through tool mapping

However, the user might be asking about something different - they might want Claude Code to understand Codex's coding conventions, API specifications, or coding style. Let me clarify this and provide a comprehensive answer.

content.text:
## Claude Code 识别 Codex 规范的情况

### 现状：已有部分支持

已安装的工具中，有 **两个** 已经内置了 Codex 兼容性：

---

### 1. Superpowers - 内置 Codex 工具映射

Superpowers 已经包含了 `codex-tools.md` 参考文件，定义了 Claude Code 和 Codex 之间的工具映射：

| Claude Code 工具 | Codex 等价物 |
|-----------------|-------------|
| `Task`（派发子代理） | `spawn_agent` |
| 多个 `Task` 调用（并行） | 多个 `spawn_agent` 调用 |
| `TodoWrite`（任务跟踪） | `update_plan` |
| `Skill`（调用技能） | 原生加载技能 |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit` | 使用原生文件工具 |
| `Bash` | 使用原生 Shell 工具 |

**环境检测**也已支持：
```bash
GIT_DIR=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
GIT_COMMON=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
```

- `GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON` → 已在链接的工作树中（跳过创建）
- `BRANCH` 为空 → 游离 HEAD（无法从沙箱创建分支/推送/PR）

---

### 2. planning-with-files - 完整的 Codex 集成

这个技能已经包含完整的 Codex 配置：

**目录结构：**
```
~/.claude/commands/planning-with-files/
├── .codex/
│   ├── hooks/           # Codex 钩子脚本
│   ├── hooks.json       # 钩子配置
│   └── skills/          # Codex 技能格式
│       └── planning-with-files/
│           └── SKILL.md
└── docs/
    └── codex.md         # Codex 安装指南
```

**支持的 Codex 生命周期钩子：**

| 钩子 | 功能 |
|------|------|
| **SessionStart** | 运行 `session-catchup.py`，注入活动计划上下文 |
| **UserPromptSubmit** | 每次用户消息时重新注入计划和最近进展 |
| **PreToolUse** | 在 Bash 前重新读取 `task_plan.md` 前 30 行 |
| **PostToolUse** | 在 Bash 活动后提醒更新 `progress.md` |
| **Stop** | 阶段未完成时阻止一次，然后回退到后续提醒 |

---

### 但是：没有专门的 "Codex 规范识别" 技能

目前 **没有** 一个专门的技能可以让 Claude Code 识别和理解 Codex 的：
- API 规范
- 编码风格
- 代码约定
- 特定语法

---

### 如果你需要 Codex 规范支持，有几种方案：

#### 方案 A：创建自定义技能（推荐）

我可以帮你创建一个专门的 Codex 规范识别技能：

```markdown
# ~/.claude/commands/codex-spec.md

识别并应用 Codex 规范

## 功能
1. 检测项目中的 Codex 配置文件
2. 读取 Codex API 规范
3. 应用 Codex 编码风格
4. 生成符合 Codex 规范的代码

## 使用方法
/codex-spec 检查当前项目的 Codex 规范
```

#### 方案 B：使用现有的 Codex 工具映射

Superpowers 已经提供了工具映射，你可以在使用 Superpowers 技能时，它会自动处理 Claude Code 和 Codex 之间的差异。

#### 方案 C：混合使用 Claude Code 和 Codex

如果你同时使用两个工具：
1. 用 Claude Code 做规划和设计（Superpowers + PRD）
2. 用 Codex 做实现（利用其原生能力）

---

### 我的建议

**如果你主要是想让 Claude Code 理解 Codex 的编码规范**，我可以帮你创建一个自定义技能。

**如果你是想在两个工具之间无缝切换**，现有的 Superpowers + planning-with-files 已经提供了足够的兼容性。

**如果你想让 Claude Code 生成符合 Codex 风格的代码**，这需要一个专门的规范定义文件。

---

你具体需要哪种 Codex 规范支持？
1. **API 规范** - 理解 Codex 的 API 调用方式
2. **编码风格** - 生成符合 Codex 风格的代码
3. **工作流兼容** - 在 Claude Code 和 Codex 之间切换
4. **其他** - 请描述你的具体需求

input.content:
# Claude Code Skills 安装与使用指南

> 最后更新：2026-05-18
> 适用于：新电脑环境配置

---

## 目录

1. [概述](#概述)
2. [已安装 Skills 列表](#已安装-skills-列表)
3. [一键安装脚本](#一键安装脚本)
4. [各 Skills 详细说明](#各-skills-详细说明)
5. [推荐使用流程](#推荐使用流程)
6. [常见问题](#常见问题)

---

## 概述

本指南记录了 Claude Code 中已安装的所有 Skills（技能），包括：

- **Superpowers** - 核心开发方法论框架（195k+ Stars）
- **PRD 生成器** - 产品需求文档生成工具
- **planning-with-files** - Manus 风格持久化规划工作流
- **Humanizer** - 去除 AI 生成文本痕迹

---

## 已安装 Skills 列表

| Skill | 来源 | Stars | 用途 |
|-------|------|-------|------|
| [Superpowers](https://github.com/obra/superpowers) | obra | 195,817 | 核心开发方法论：TDD、调试、协作 |
| [PRD 生成器](https://github.com/brezzashen/claude-code-prd-skill) | brezzashen | 46 | 产品需求文档生成（中英双语） |
| [planning-with-files](https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files) | OthmanAdi | 21,531 | Manus 风格持久化 Markdown 规划 |
| [Humanizer](https://github.com/blader/humanizer) | blader | 19,413 | 去除 AI 生成文本痕迹 |

---

## 一键安装脚本

### Windows (PowerShell)

```powershell
# 创建目录
mkdir -Force ~/.claude/commands

# 安装 Superpowers
git clone https://github.com/obra/superpowers.git ~/.claude/superpowers

# 安装 PRD 生成器
curl -L https://github.com/brezzashen/claude-code-prd-skill/archive/main.tar.gz | tar -xz --strip-components=2 -C ~/.claude/commands claude-code-prd-skill-main/commands

# 安装 planning-with-files
git clone https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files.git ~/.claude/commands/planning-with-files

# 安装 Humanizer
git clone https://github.com/blader/humanizer.git ~/.claude/commands/humanizer

Write-Host "安装完成！"
```

### macOS / Linux (Bash)

```bash
# 创建目录
mkdir -p ~/.claude/commands

# 安装 Superpowers
git clone https://github.com/obra/superpowers.git ~/.claude/superpowers

# 安装 PRD 生成器
curl -L https://github.com/brezzashen/claude-code-prd-skill/archive/main.tar.gz | tar -xz --strip-components=2 -C ~/.claude/commands claude-code-prd-skill-main/commands

# 安装 planning-with-files
git clone https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files.git ~/.claude/commands/planning-with-files

# 安装 Humanizer
git clone https://github.com/blader/humanizer.git ~/.claude/commands/humanizer

echo "安装完成！"
```

---

## 各 Skills 详细说明

### 1. Superpowers

**仓库：** https://github.com/obra/superpowers

**核心功能：**
- 20+ 经过实战验证的技能
- TDD（测试驱动开发）
- 系统化调试
- 代码审查
- Git 工作树管理

**主要斜杠命令：**

| 命令 | 用途 | 使用场景 |
|------|------|----------|
| `/brainstorm` | 头脑风暴 | 任何创意工作之前 |
| `/write-plan` | 写实施计划 | 有了设计后 |
| `/execute-plan` | 执行计划 | 有了计划后 |

**核心工作流程：**

```
需求 → /brainstorm → /write-plan → /execute-plan → 完成
```

**关键原则：**
- TDD：没有失败的测试，就没有生产代码
- 系统化调试：没有根因调查，就没有修复
- 完成前验证：没有新鲜验证证据，就没有完成声明

---

### 2. PRD 生成器

**仓库：** https://github.com/brezzashen/claude-code-prd-skill

**核心功能：**
- 生成完整的产品需求文档
- 中英双语支持
- 架构图（Mermaid）
- 验收场景（GWT 格式）
- 生命周期管理

**主要斜杠命令：**

| 命令 | 用途 |
|------|------|
| `/prd` | 生成 PRD（3 个文件） |
| `/flow` | 在生命周期阶段间流转 |
| `/build` | 读取 PRD，派发并行团队代理 |
| `/archive` | 归档完成的工作 |
| `/pipeline` | 一键运行整个生命周期 |

**生成的文件结构：**

```
docs/prd/01_feature_name/
├── proposal.md    # 产品提案 + 架构图（中英双语）
├── baseline.md    # 当前状态 + 变更规格
└── tasks.md       # 高层任务列表
```

**生命周期阶段：**

```
prd → in_progress → testing → completed
```

**使用示例：**

```bash
# 生成 PRD
/prd 用户登录系统 - 支持邮箱密码登录和 Google OAuth

# 流转到下一阶段
/flow 用户登录系统 in_progress

# 归档
/archive 用户登录系统
```

---

### 3. planning-with-files

**仓库：** https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files

**核心功能：**
- Manus 风格持久化规划
- 三个核心文件：`task_plan.md`、`findings.md`、`progress.md`
- 生命周期钩子自动注入上下文
- 支持 Claude Code、Codex、Cursor

**三个核心文件：**

| 文件 | 用途 | 位置 |
|------|------|------|
| `task_plan.md` | 阶段、进展、决策 | 项目根目录 |
| `findings.md` | 研究、发现 | 项目根目录 |
| `progress.md` | 会话日志、测试结果 | 项目根目录 |

**生命周期钩子：**

| 钩子 | 功能 |
|------|------|
| SessionStart | 运行 session-catchup.py，注入活动计划上下文 |
| UserPromptSubmit | 每次用户消息时重新注入计划和最近进展 |
| PreToolUse | 在 Bash 前重新读取 task_plan.md 前 30 行 |
| PostToolUse | 在 Bash 活动后提醒更新 progress.md |
| Stop | 阶段未完成时阻止一次，然后回退到后续提醒 |

**Codex 集成：**

planning-with-files 已包含完整的 Codex 配置：
- `.codex/skills/` - Codex 技能格式
- `.codex/hooks.json` - 钩子配置
- `.codex/hooks/` - 钩子脚本

---

### 4. Humanizer

**仓库：** https://github.com/blader/humanizer

**核心功能：**
- 去除 AI 生成的文本痕迹
- 让内容更自然、更人性化
- 保持技术准确性

**使用方法：**

```bash
# 在对话中调用
/humanizer
```

---

## 推荐使用流程

### 方案 A：PRD → Superpowers（正式项目）

适合：正式项目、团队协作、需要文档沉淀

```
阶段 1：产品规划（PRD 生成器）
  /prd 用户登录系统 - 支持邮箱密码和 OAuth
  ↓
  产出：docs/prd/01_user_login/
    - proposal.md    （产品提案 + 架构图）
    - baseline.md    （当前状态 + 变更规格）
    - tasks.md       （高层任务列表）
  ↓
阶段 2：开发执行（Superpowers）
  /execute-plan
  ↓
  产出：代码 + 测试 + PR
  ↓
阶段 3：收尾（PRD 流程管理）
  /flow 用户登录系统 in_progress
  /flow 用户登录系统 testing
  /flow 用户登录系统 completed
  /archive
```

**优点：**
- 有完整的产品文档沉淀
- 架构决策有记录
- 验收标准清晰
- 适合团队协作和代码审查

---

### 方案 B：Superpowers 全流程（个人项目）

适合：个人项目、快速迭代、探索性开发

```
/brainstorm 我想做一个用户登录系统
  ↓
  产出：设计文档
  ↓
/write-plan
  ↓
  产出：实施计划
  ↓
/execute-plan
  ↓
  产出：代码 + 测试
```

**优点：**
- 流程更简洁
- 更灵活，可以快速调整
- 适合小功能和快速实验

---

### 方案 C：混合使用（推荐）

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     PRD 生成器                               │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ • 产品需求分析                                        │   │
│  │ • 架构设计（Mermaid 图）                              │   │
│  │ • 变更规格（ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED）                  │   │
│  │ • 验收场景（GWT 格式）                                │   │
│  │ • 任务分解（高层级）                                  │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ↓
                    【重叠区域：任务规划】
                              ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     Superpowers                              │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ • 头脑风暴（设计探索）                                │   │
│  │ • 实施计划（详细步骤 + 代码）                         │   │
│  │ • TDD 执行（测试驱动开发）                            │   │
│  │ • 系统化调试                                          │   │
│  │ • 完成前验证                                          │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**使用建议：**

| 场景 | 推荐工具 |
|------|---------|
| 正式项目、需要文档 | PRD → Superpowers |
| 个人项目、快速迭代 | Superpowers 全流程 |
| 团队协作、需要审查 | PRD → Superpowers |
| 探索性开发、不确定需求 | Superpowers 全流程 |
| 需要中英双语文档 | PRD 生成器 |
| 需要详细的代码级计划 | Superpowers |

---

## Superpowers 核心技能详解

### TDD（测试驱动开发）

**核心原则：**
```
没有失败的测试，就没有生产代码
```

**Red-Green-Refactor 循环：**

1. **RED** - 写一个失败的测试
2. **验证 RED** - 确认测试失败（必须！）
3. **GREEN** - 写最简代码让测试通过
4. **验证 GREEN** - 确认测试通过
5. **REFACTOR** - 清理代码，保持测试绿色

**示例：**

```typescript
// RED - 写失败的测试
test('rejects empty email', async () => {
  const result = await submitForm({ email: '' });
  expect(result.error).toBe('Email required');
});

// 验证 RED
// $ npm test
// FAIL: expected 'Email required', got undefined

// GREEN - 写最简实现
function submitForm(data: FormData) {
  if (!data.email?.trim()) {
    return { error: 'Email required' };
  }
  // ...
}

// 验证 GREEN
// $ npm test
// PASS
```

---

### 系统化调试

**核心原则：**
```
没有根因调查，就没有修复
```

**四个阶段：**

1. **根因调查** - 读错误信息、重现、检查最近更改、收集证据
2. **模式分析** - 找工作示例、对比、识别差异
3. **假设和测试** - 形成单一假设、最小测试
4. **实现** - 创建失败测试、实现单一修复、验证

**关键原则：**
- 不要猜测，要调查
- 一次只改一个变量
- 如果 3 次修复都失败了，质疑架构

---

### 完成前验证

**核心原则：**
```
没有新鲜验证证据，就没有完成声明
```

**门控函数：**

1. **识别** - 什么命令能证明这个声明？
2. **运行** - 执行完整命令（新鲜、完整）
3. **读取** - 完整输出，检查退出码，计数失败
4. **验证** - 输出是否确认声明？
5. **只有这时** - 才能做声明

**常见错误：**

| 声称 | 需要 | 不足够 |
|------|------|--------|
| 测试通过 | 测试命令输出：0 失败 | 上次运行、"应该通过" |
| 构建成功 | 构建命令：exit 0 | Linter 通过、日志看起来好 |
| Bug 修复 | 测试原始症状：通过 | 代码改了、假设修复了 |

---

## 快速参考卡

| 场景 | 使用的技能 |
|------|-----------|
| 开始新功能 | `/brainstorm` |
| 有了设计后 | `/write-plan` |
| 有了计划后 | `/execute-plan` |
| 写代码时 | TDD（自动触发） |
| 遇到 bug | 系统化调试（自动触发） |
| 声称完成前 | 完成前验证（自动触发） |
| 做 PRD | `/prd` |
| 去 AI 痕迹 | `/humanizer` |

---

## 常见问题

### Q1: Superpowers 和 PRD 生成器有什么区别？

**A:** 
- **PRD 生成器** 关注 "做什么" - 产品思维、需求分析、架构设计
- **Superpowers** 关注 "怎么做" - 实现方法论、代码质量、TDD

两者可以串联使用：PRD 做规划，Superpowers 做执行。

---

### Q2: 我应该先用哪个？

**A:** 
- 如果是正式项目，先用 `/prd` 做产品规划
- 如果是个人项目或探索性开发，直接用 `/brainstorm`

---

### Q3: planning-with-files 和 Superpowers 的 `/write-plan` 有什么区别？

**A:**
- **planning-with-files** 是持久化的 Markdown 文件，适合长期项目
- **Superpowers 的 `/write-plan`** 是会话内的计划，适合单次任务

两者可以配合使用：planning-with-files 做长期规划，Superpowers 做具体任务。

---

### Q4: 如何在新电脑上恢复环境？

**A:** 运行本文档中的一键安装脚本即可。

---

### Q5: 这些 Skills 会自动更新吗？

**A:** 不会。如果需要更新，需要手动拉取最新代码：

```bash
# 更新 Superpowers
cd ~/.claude/superpowers && git pull

# 更新 planning-with-files
cd ~/.claude/commands/planning-with-files && git pull

# 更新 Humanizer
cd ~/.claude/commands/humanizer && git pull

# PRD 生成器需要重新下载
curl -L https://github.com/brezzashen/claude-code-prd-skill/archive/main.tar.gz | tar -xz --strip-components=2 -C ~/.claude/commands claude-code-prd-skill-main/commands
```

> 最后更新：2026-05-18
> 适用于：新电脑环境配置

---

## 目录

1. [概述](#概述)
2. [已安装 Skills 列表](#已安装-skills-列表)
3. [一键安装脚本](#一键安装脚本)
4. [各 Skills 详细说明](#各-skills-详细说明)
5. [推荐使用流程](#推荐使用流程)
6. [常见问题](#常见问题)

---

## 概述

本指南记录了 Claude Code 中已安装的所有 Skills（技能），包括：

- **Superpowers** - 核心开发方法论框架（195k+ Stars）
- **PRD 生成器** - 产品需求文档生成工具
- **planning-with-files** - Manus 风格持久化规划工作流
- **Humanizer** - 去除 AI 生成文本痕迹

---

## 已安装 Skills 列表

| Skill | 来源 | Stars | 用途 |
|-------|------|-------|------|
| [Superpowers](https://github.com/obra/superpowers) | obra | 195,817 | 核心开发方法论：TDD、调试、协作 |
| [PRD 生成器](https://github.com/brezzashen/claude-code-prd-skill) | brezzashen | 46 | 产品需求文档生成（中英双语） |
| [planning-with-files](https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files) | OthmanAdi | 21,531 | Manus 风格持久化 Markdown 规划 |
| [Humanizer](https://github.com/blader/humanizer) | blader | 19,413 | 去除 AI 生成文本痕迹 |

## 一键安装脚本

### Windows (PowerShell)

```powershell
# 创建目录
mkdir -Force ~/.claude/commands

# 安装 Superpowers
git clone https://github.com/obra/superpowers.git ~/.claude/superpowers

# 安装 PRD 生成器
curl -L https://github.com/brezzashen/claude-code-prd-skill/archive/main.tar.gz | tar -xz --strip-components=2 -C ~/.claude/commands claude-code-prd-skill-main/commands

# 安装 planning-with-files
git clone https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files.git ~/.claude/commands/planning-with-files

# 安装 Humanizer
git clone https://github.com/blader/humanizer.git ~/.claude/commands/humanizer

Write-Host "安装完成！"
```

### macOS / Linux (Bash)

```bash
# 创建目录
mkdir -p ~/.claude/commands

# 安装 Superpowers
git clone https://github.com/obra/superpowers.git ~/.claude/superpowers

# 安装 PRD 生成器
curl -L https://github.com/brezzashen/claude-code-prd-skill/archive/main.tar.gz | tar -xz --strip-components=2 -C ~/.claude/commands claude-code-prd-skill-main/commands

# 安装 planning-with-files
git clone https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files.git ~/.claude/commands/planning-with-files

# 安装 Humanizer
git clone https://github.com/blader/humanizer.git ~/.claude/commands/humanizer

echo "安装完成！"
```

---

## 各 Skills 详细说明

### 1. Superpowers

**仓库：** https://github.com/obra/superpowers

**核心功能：**
- 20+ 经过实战验证的技能
- TDD（测试驱动开发）
- 系统化调试
- 代码审查
- Git 工作树管理

**主要斜杠命令：**

| 命令 | 用途 | 使用场景 |
|------|------|----------|
| `/brainstorm` | 头脑风暴 | 任何创意工作之前 |
| `/write-plan` | 写实施计划 | 有了设计后 |
| `/execute-plan` | 执行计划 | 有了计划后 |

**核心工作流程：**

```
需求 → /brainstorm → /write-plan → /execute-plan → 完成
```

**关键原则：**
- TDD：没有失败的测试，就没有生产代码
- 系统化调试：没有根因调查，就没有修复
- 完成前验证：没有新鲜验证证据，就没有完成声明

---

### 2. PRD 生成器

**仓库：** https://github.com/brezzashen/claude-code-prd-skill

**核心功能：**
- 生成完整的产品需求文档
- 中英双语支持
- 架构图（Mermaid）
- 验收场景（GWT 格式）
- 生命周期管理

**主要斜杠命令：**

| 命令 | 用途 |
|------|------|
| `/prd` | 生成 PRD（3 个文件） |
| `/flow` | 在生命周期阶段间流转 |
| `/build` | 读取 PRD，派发并行团队代理 |
| `/archive` | 归档完成的工作 |
| `/pipeline` | 一键运行整个生命周期 |

**生成的文件结构：**

```
docs/prd/01_feature_name/
├── proposal.md    # 产品提案 + 架构图（中英双语）
├── baseline.md    # 当前状态 + 变更规格
└── tasks.md       # 高层任务列表
```

**生命周期阶段：**

```
prd → in_progress → testing → completed
```

**使用示例：**

```bash
# 生成 PRD
/prd 用户登录系统 - 支持邮箱密码登录和 Google OAuth

# 流转到下一阶段
/flow 用户登录系统 in_progress

# 归档
/archive 用户登录系统
```

---

### 3. planning-with-files

**仓库：** https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files

**核心功能：**
- Manus 风格持久化规划
- 三个核心文件：`task_plan.md`、`findings.md`、`progress.md`
- 生命周期钩子自动注入上下文
- 支持 Claude Code、Codex、Cursor

**三个核心文件：**

| 文件 | 用途 | 位置 |
|------|------|------|
| `task_plan.md` | 阶段、进展、决策 | 项目根目录 |
| `findings.md` | 研究、发现 | 项目根目录 |
| `progress.md` | 会话日志、测试结果 | 项目根目录 |

**生命周期钩子：**

| 钩子 | 功能 |
|------|------|
| SessionStart | 运行 session-catchup.py，注入活动计划上下文 |
| UserPromptSubmit | 每次用户消息时重新注入计划和最近进展 |
| PreToolUse | 在 Bash 前重新读取 task_plan.md 前 30 行 |
| PostToolUse | 在 Bash 活动后提醒更新 progress.md |
| Stop | 阶段未完成时阻止一次，然后回退到后续提醒 |

**Codex 集成：**

planning-with-files 已包含完整的 Codex 配置：
- `.codex/skills/` - Codex 技能格式
- `.codex/hooks.json` - 钩子配置
- `.codex/hooks/` - 钩子脚本

---

### 4. Humanizer

**仓库：** https://github.com/blader/humanizer

**核心功能：**
- 去除 AI 生成的文本痕迹
- 让内容更自然、更人性化
- 保持技术准确性

**使用方法：**

```bash
# 在对话中调用
/humanizer
```

---

## 推荐使用流程

### 方案 A：PRD → Superpowers（正式项目）

适合：正式项目、团队协作、需要文档沉淀

```
阶段 1：产品规划（PRD 生成器）
  /prd 用户登录系统 - 支持邮箱密码和 OAuth
  ↓
  产出：docs/prd/01_user_login/
    - proposal.md    （产品提案 + 架构图）
    - baseline.md    （当前状态 + 变更规格）
    - tasks.md       （高层任务列表）
  ↓
阶段 2：开发执行（Superpowers）
  /execute-plan
  ↓
  产出：代码 + 测试 + PR
  ↓
阶段 3：收尾（PRD 流程管理）
  /flow 用户登录系统 in_progress
  /flow 用户登录系统 testing
  /flow 用户登录系统 completed
  /archive
```

**优点：**
- 有完整的产品文档沉淀
- 架构决策有记录
- 验收标准清晰
- 适合团队协作和代码审查

---

### 方案 B：Superpowers 全流程（个人项目）

适合：个人项目、快速迭代、探索性开发

```
/brainstorm 我想做一个用户登录系统
  ↓
  产出：设计文档
  ↓
/write-plan
  ↓
  产出：实施计划
  ↓
/execute-plan
  ↓
  产出：代码 + 测试
```

**优点：**
- 流程更简洁
- 更灵活，可以快速调整
- 适合小功能和快速实验

---

### 方案 C：混合使用（推荐）

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     PRD 生成器                               │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ • 产品需求分析                                        │   │
│  │ • 架构设计（Mermaid 图）                              │   │
│  │ • 变更规格（ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED）                  │   │
│  │ • 验收场景（GWT 格式）                                │   │
│  │ • 任务分解（高层级）                                  │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ↓
                    【重叠区域：任务规划】
                              ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     Superpowers                              │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ • 头脑风暴（设计探索）                                │   │
│  │ • 实施计划（详细步骤 + 代码）                         │   │
│  │ • TDD 执行（测试驱动开发）                            │   │
│  │ • 系统化调试                                          │   │
│  │ • 完成前验证                                          │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**使用建议：**

| 场景 | 推荐工具 |
|------|---------|
| 正式项目、需要文档 | PRD → Superpowers |
| 个人项目、快速迭代 | Superpowers 全流程 |
| 团队协作、需要审查 | PRD → Superpowers |
| 探索性开发、不确定需求 | Superpowers 全流程 |
| 需要中英双语文档 | PRD 生成器 |
| 需要详细的代码级计划 | Superpowers |

---

## Superpowers 核心技能详解

### TDD（测试驱动开发）

**核心原则：**
```
没有失败的测试，就没有生产代码
```

**Red-Green-Refactor 循环：**

1. **RED** - 写一个失败的测试
2. **验证 RED** - 确认测试失败（必须！）
3. **GREEN** - 写最简代码让测试通过
4. **验证 GREEN** - 确认测试通过
5. **REFACTOR** - 清理代码，保持测试绿色

**示例：**

```typescript
// RED - 写失败的测试
test('rejects empty email', async () => {
  const result = await submitForm({ email: '' });
  expect(result.error).toBe('Email required');
});

// 验证 RED
// $ npm test
// FAIL: expected 'Email required', got undefined

// GREEN - 写最简实现
function submitForm(data: FormData) {
  if (!data.email?.trim()) {
    return { error: 'Email required' };
  }
  // ...
}

// 验证 GREEN
// $ npm test
// PASS
```

---

### 系统化调试

**核心原则：**
```
没有根因调查，就没有修复
```

**四个阶段：**

1. **根因调查** - 读错误信息、重现、检查最近更改、收集证据
2. **模式分析** - 找工作示例、对比、识别差异
3. **假设和测试** - 形成单一假设、最小测试
4. **实现** - 创建失败测试、实现单一修复、验证

**关键原则：**
- 不要猜测，要调查
- 一次只改一个变量
- 如果 3 次修复都失败了，质疑架构

---

### 完成前验证

**核心原则：**
```
没有新鲜验证证据，就没有完成声明
```

**门控函数：**

1. **识别** - 什么命令能证明这个声明？
2. **运行** - 执行完整命令（新鲜、完整）
3. **读取** - 完整输出，检查退出码，计数失败
4. **验证** - 输出是否确认声明？
5. **只有这时** - 才能做声明

**常见错误：**

| 声称 | 需要 | 不足够 |
|------|------|--------|
| 测试通过 | 测试命令输出：0 失败 | 上次运行、"应该通过" |
| 构建成功 | 构建命令：exit 0 | Linter 通过、日志看起来好 |
| Bug 修复 | 测试原始症状：通过 | 代码改了、假设修复了 |

---

## 快速参考卡

|------|-----------|
| 开始新功能 | `/brainstorm` |

| 声称完成前 | 完成前验证（自动触发） |
| 做 PRD | `/prd` |
| 去 AI 痕迹 | `/humanizer` |

---

## 常见问题

### Q1: Superpowers 和 PRD 生成器有什么区别？

**A:** 
- **PRD 生成器** 关注 "做什么" - 产品思维、需求分析、架构设计

两者可以串联使用：PRD 做规划，Superpowers 做执行。

---

### Q2: 我应该先用哪个？

**A:** 
- 如果是正式项目，先用 `/prd` 做产品规划
- 如果是个人项目或探索性开发，直接用 `/brainstorm`

---

### Q3: planning-with-files 和 Superpowers 的 `/write-plan` 有什么区别？

**A:**
- **planning-with-files** 是持久化的 Markdown 文件，适合长期项目
- **Superpowers 的 `/write-plan`** 是会话内的计划，适合单次任务

两者可以配合使用：planning-with-files 做长期规划，Superpowers 做具体任务。

---

### Q4: 如何在新电脑上恢复环境？

**A:** 运行本文档中的一键安装脚本即可。

---

### Q5: 这些 Skills 会自动更新吗？

**A:** 不会。如果需要更新，需要手动拉取最新代码：

```bash

cd ~/.claude/superpowers && git pull

# 更新 planning-with-files
```

input.content:
# Claude Code Skills Agent Guide

> Type: agent-instruction
> Version: 2026-05-18
> Purpose: Skills installation, usage, and workflow for AI agents

---

## Installation

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.claude/commands

# Superpowers
git clone https://github.com/obra/superpowers.git ~/.claude/superpowers

# PRD Generator
curl -L https://github.com/brezzashen/claude-code-prd-skill/archive/main.tar.gz | tar -xz --strip-components=2 -C ~/.claude/commands claude-code-prd-skill-main/commands

# planning-with-files
git clone https://github.com/OthmanAdi/planning-with-files.git ~/.claude/commands/planning-with-files

# Humanizer
git clone https://github.com/blader/humanizer.git ~/.claude/commands/humanizer
```

---

## Skills Registry

### Superpowers

- Path: `~/.claude/superpowers/`
- Type: methodology-framework
- Triggers: before any creative work, implementation, debugging, completion claims

Commands:
- `/brainstorm` - Design exploration before implementation
- `/write-plan` - Create implementation plan from spec
- `/execute-plan` - Execute plan with TDD

Auto-triggered skills:
- `test-driven-development` - When writing code
- `systematic-debugging` - When encountering bugs
- `verification-before-completion` - Before claiming work done

### PRD Generator

- Path: `~/.claude/commands/prd.md`
- Type: product-requirements
- Triggers: when user requests PRD, product spec, requirements doc

Commands:
- `/prd <name> - <description>` - Generate PRD (3 files)
- `/flow <name> [phase]` - Transition phase: prd → in_progress → testing → completed
- `/build <name>` - Execute PRD with parallel agents
- `/archive <name>` - Archive completed work
- `/pipeline <name> - <description>` - Full lifecycle in one command

Output:
```
docs/prd/{seq}_{name}/
├── proposal.md    # EN/ZH bilingual, Mermaid diagrams
├── baseline.md    # Current state + change spec (ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED)
└── tasks.md       # Phased task list
```

### planning-with-files

- Path: `~/.claude/commands/planning-with-files/`
- Type: persistent-planning
- Triggers: long-term projects, multi-session work

Output files (project root):
- `task_plan.md` - Phases, progress, decisions
- `findings.md` - Research, discoveries
- `progress.md` - Session log, test results

### Humanizer

- Path: `~/.claude/commands/humanizer/`
- Type: text-processing
- Triggers: when user requests natural text, remove AI traces

Commands:
- `/humanizer` - Remove AI-generated text patterns

---

## Workflow Decision Tree

```
User request received
│
├─ Is it a new feature/product?
│  ├─ Yes, formal project → /prd → /flow → /execute-plan → /flow → /archive
│  ├─ Yes, personal project → /brainstorm → /write-plan → /execute-plan
│  └─ Yes, quick experiment → /brainstorm → implement directly
│
├─ Is it a bug fix?
│  └─ Use systematic-debugging → TDD → verification-before-completion
│
├─ Is it a text refinement?
│  └─ Use /humanizer
│
└─ Is it planning/research?
   └─ Use /brainstorm or planning-with-files
```

---

## Workflow: PRD → Superpowers (Formal Projects)

Step 1: Generate PRD
```
/prd <feature_name> - <description>
```
Output: `docs/prd/{seq}_{feature_name}/`

Step 2: Transition to development
```
/flow <feature_name> in_progress
```

Step 3: Execute with Superpowers
```
/execute-plan
```
Uses TDD, systematic debugging, verification before completion.

Step 4: Transition to testing
```
/flow <feature_name> testing
```

Step 5: Complete and archive
```
/flow <feature_name> completed
/archive <feature_name>
```

---

## Workflow: Superpowers Only (Personal Projects)

Step 1: Brainstorm
```
/brainstorm <idea>
```
Output: Design doc at `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`

Step 2: Write plan
```
/write-plan
```
Output: Plan at `docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`

Step 3: Execute plan
```
/execute-plan
```
Auto-applies TDD, systematic debugging, verification.

---

## Superpowers Core Skills (Auto-triggered)

### test-driven-development

Trigger: Before writing any production code

Process:
1. RED: Write failing test
2. Verify RED: Confirm test fails
3. GREEN: Write minimal code to pass
4. Verify GREEN: Confirm test passes
5. REFACTOR: Clean up, keep tests green

Rule: No production code without a failing test first.

### systematic-debugging

Trigger: Any bug, test failure, unexpected behavior

Phases:
1. Root Cause Investigation - Read errors, reproduce, check changes, gather evidence
2. Pattern Analysis - Find working examples, compare, identify differences
3. Hypothesis and Testing - Form single hypothesis, test minimally
4. Implementation - Create failing test, implement single fix, verify

Rule: No fixes without root cause investigation first.

### verification-before-completion

Trigger: Before any completion/success claims

Process:
1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute full command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm claim?
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Rule: No completion claims without fresh verification evidence.

---

## Tool Mapping (Claude Code ↔ Codex)

| Claude Code | Codex |
|-------------|-------|
| `Task` (dispatch subagent) | `spawn_agent` |
| Multiple `Task` calls | Multiple `spawn_agent` calls |
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Skill` | Native skill loading |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit` | Native file tools |
| `Bash` | Native shell tools |

Codex config requirement:
```toml
# ~/.codex/config.toml
[features]
multi_agent = true
codex_hooks = true
```

---

## Environment Detection

For worktree-aware skills:

```bash
GIT_DIR=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
GIT_COMMON=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
```

Decision matrix:
- `GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON` → In linked worktree (skip creation)
- `BRANCH` empty → Detached HEAD (cannot branch/push/PR from sandbox)
- `GIT_DIR == GIT_COMMON` → Normal environment (full behavior)

---

## Quick Reference

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| New feature, formal | `/prd` → `/flow` → `/execute-plan` → `/flow` → `/archive` |
| New feature, personal | `/brainstorm` → `/write-plan` → `/execute-plan` |
| Bug fix | systematic-debugging → TDD → verification |
| Text refinement | `/humanizer` |
| Design exploration | `/brainstorm` |
| Long-term project | planning-with-files |
| Claim work done | Run verification first |

---

## File Locations

| Item | Path |
|------|------|
| Superpowers | `~/.claude/superpowers/` |
| PRD commands | `~/.claude/commands/prd.md`, `flow.md`, `build.md`, `archive.md`, `pipeline.md` |
| planning-with-files | `~/.claude/commands/planning-with-files/` |
| Humanizer | `~/.claude/commands/humanizer/` |
| PRD output | `docs/prd/{seq}_{name}/` |
| Design specs | `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` |
| Implementation plans | `docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md` |

---

## Update Commands

```bash
cd ~/.claude/superpowers && git pull
cd ~/.claude/commands/planning-with-files && git pull
cd ~/.claude/commands/humanizer && git pull
curl -L https://github.com/brezzashen/claude-code-prd-skill/archive/main.tar.gz | tar -xz --strip-components=2 -C ~/.claude/commands claude-code-prd-skill-main/commands
```

1	# Ron Group Lead Research MVP
2	
3	<!-- CODEx_START: project_index -->
4	*Updated: 2026-05-12 12:10*
5	
6	## Purpose
7	
8	This project is a local MVP for Ron Group to collect, organize, review, and enrich potential B2B customer leads in the restaurant and hospitality industry.
9	
10	The main goal is lead collection and contact enrichment for future outreach. The dashboard is now a lightweight manual fill tool for reviewing country-level lead gaps and exporting updated CSV files.
11	
12	The current active focus is Australia first. The near-term workflow should prioritize Australia restaurant and hotel final customers, key-person contact discovery, and a repeatable process that can later become a local tool.
13	
14	The CSV and dashboard now separate company-level contact channels from key contact person information so outreach readiness is easier to judge. The current dashboard should stay simple: country filter first, then manual filling of missing company contact, form, and key-person fields.
15	
16	## Folder Structure
17	
18	```text
19	project-folder/
20	  README.md
21	  data/
22	    leads.csv
23	    contacts.csv
24	    search_keywords.csv
25	    keyword_runs.csv
26	    outreach_log.csv
27	  dashboard/
28	    index.html
29	    app.js
30	    style.css
31	  docs/
32	    project-overview.md
33	    lead-collection-workflow.md
34	    lead-enrichment-workflow.md
35	    lead-table-fields.md
36	    dashboard-guide.md
37	    iteration-setup.md
38	    cli-operating-rules.md
39	    request-solution-log.md
40	    current-progress.md
41	  backup/
42	  exports/
43	  reports/
44	  skills/
45	```
46	
47	## Specialized Documentation
48	
49	- `docs/project-overview.md`: Business background, target customers, and MVP scope.
50	- `docs/lead-collection-workflow.md`: Lead discovery workflow and public-source rules.
51	- `docs/lead-enrichment-workflow.md`: Contact enrichment workflow and stopping rules.
52	- `docs/lead-table-fields.md`: `leads.csv` fields, statuses, scoring, and value rules.
53	- `docs/dashboard-guide.md`: Local country-based dashboard fill/export workflow.
54	- `docs/iteration-setup.md`: Backups, timestamps, update tags, and rollback.
55	- `docs/cli-operating-rules.md`: Which files Codex should read for each task type.
56	- `docs/request-solution-log.md`: Concise record of important user requests and CLI/Codex handling plans.
57	- `docs/current-progress.md`: Current project state, next work, and commands for resuming.
58	
59	## Current Data Files
60	
61	- `data/leads.csv`: Lead records and enrichment status.
62	- `data/contacts.csv`: Person-level contact records linked to `leads.csv` by `lead_id`.
63	- `data/search_keywords.csv`: Search keyword templates for lead discovery.
64	- `data/keyword_runs.csv`: Keyword run and batch tracking.
65	- `data/outreach_log.csv`: Future outreach conflict-prevention template. Do not fill it until outreach is actually planned or sent.
66	
67	## Project Tools
68	
69	- `dashboard/index.html`, `dashboard/app.js`, `dashboard/style.css`: Manual fill dashboard for reviewing country-level lead gaps, editing company/contact fields, and exporting canonical CSV files.
70	- `scripts/start_dashboard.ps1`: Starts the local dashboard server so the browser can read default CSV files.
71	- `scripts/build_au_review_queue.py`: Auxiliary historical Australia review queue; this segmentation path is paused unless explicitly requested.
72	- `scripts/build_form_kp_candidate_queue.py`: Builds the current Australia form/KP queue from existing CSV data and marks missing form, KP, or direct-contact gaps.
73	- `scripts/generate_kp_search_tasks.py`: Builds direct KP search-task queues from existing leads, prioritizing Australia restaurant/hotel final-customer candidates, known KP names, official-site queries, PDF/news queries, and LinkedIn manual-review entry points.
74	- `scripts/extract_public_contact_candidates.py`: Uses Scrapling static, dynamic, or stealth fetching to extract candidate emails, phones, contact/team links, LinkedIn URLs, and role snippets from queued official websites. Supports one-level same-domain link follow-up and outputs candidate evidence for CSV registration with confidence/status notes.
75	- `scripts/check_capability_inventory.py`: Checks whether the current computer has the project capabilities needed for the current workflow and suggests setup commands when something is missing.
76	- `requirements.txt`: Python package inventory for recreating the project-local extraction environment.
77	- Project-local `.venv`: Preferred Python environment for newly installed tools. Do not use global C: drive installs when the tool can run from the project directory.
78	- Project-local `.ms-playwright`: Preferred Playwright browser directory. Use `PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH` to keep browser binaries outside C: when possible.
79	- Project-local `.codex/tools/mcp-notifications`: Node/npm MCP server used for lightweight desktop notifications from Codex. `node_modules` is ignored; reinstall with `npm.cmd install --prefix .codex\tools\mcp-notifications @topvisor/mcp-notifications` if needed.
80	- Scrapling `Fetcher`: Static public-page extraction to reduce manual page reading and token usage. Installed in `.venv`.
81	- Scrapling `DynamicFetcher`: Browser extraction for pages that need JavaScript rendering. Uses project-local Playwright Chromium.
82	- Scrapling `StealthyFetcher`: Stealth browser extraction for approved high-capability runs with anti-ban controls. Uses project-local Playwright/Patchright dependencies.
83	- Playwright / Chromium: Browser runtime used by Scrapling dynamic and stealth fetchers. Installed under `.ms-playwright`.
84	- `data/search_keywords.csv` and `data/keyword_runs.csv`: Keyword and batch-control tracking for repeatable discovery tests.
85	- `reports/*.md` and `reports/*.csv`: Batch outputs, candidate queues, test results, and evidence summaries.
86	- `docs/request-solution-log.md`: Required task-end log for important requests, tool decisions, workflow changes, file-changing tasks, and future operating context.
87	- `.codex/config.toml` and `.codex/hooks/*.py`: Project-local Codex hooks for selected guardrails: destructive command blocking, request-log reminders, current-progress threshold warnings, script compile checks, capability inventory reminders, high-capability extraction scope warnings, and UTF-8 Markdown checks.
88	
89	## Capability Setup
90	
91	Use the project-local environment first:
92	
93	```powershell
94	cd E:\AI\Codex\TestProject
95	python -m venv .venv
96	.\.venv\Scripts\python -m pip install -r requirements.txt
97	$env:PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH='E:\AI\Codex\TestProject\.ms-playwright'
98	.\.venv\Scripts\python -m playwright install chromium
99	.\.venv\Scripts\python scripts\check_capability_inventory.py
100	```
101	
102	When moving to another computer, run the inventory check first. If a task fails repeatedly and the existing tools are not enough, evaluate adding a new tool, skill, library, or language and record it in this section plus `docs/request-solution-log.md`.
103	
104	## Current Operating Priority
105	
106	- Market: Australia first.
107	- Customer focus: restaurant and hotel final customers first.
108	- Contact focus: key-person email first, key-person phone second, company-level contact paths after that.
109	- Tool goal: turn the proven Australia search, scoring, KP enrichment, CSV update, and reporting workflow into a repeatable local tool.
110	
111	## Resume This Project
112	
113	From PowerShell:
114	
115	```powershell
116	cd E:\AI\Codex\TestProject
117	git pull
118	codex "Read README.md, docs/current-progress.md, docs/request-solution-log.md, docs/cli-operating-rules.md, docs/lead-enrichment-workflow.md, and docs/lead-collection-workflow.md first. Then continue the current Ron Group Australia restaurant/hotel lead toolization work. You may iterate automatically inside the approved task scope, but do not start a new business collection or enrichment iteration beyond that scope without explicit approval."
119	```
120	
121	## Local Dashboard
122	
123	Start the dashboard through the local server so the browser can read the default CSV files:
124	
125	```powershell
126	cd E:\AI\Codex\TestProject
127	.\scripts\start_dashboard.ps1
128	```
129	
130	Then use `http://localhost:8765/dashboard/`. The dashboard supports manual filling of missing company contact, form, key-person, and all linked contact fields, then exports canonical `leads.csv` and `contacts.csv` files with editor name, timestamp, and change notes. It does not collect leads, enrich contacts automatically, send emails, log in to platforms, or call AI APIs.
131	
132	<!-- CODEx_END: project_index -->
133

# Ron Group Lead Research MVP

<!-- CODEx_START: project_index -->
*Updated: 2026-05-12 12:10*

## Purpose

This project is a local MVP for Ron Group to collect, organize, review, and enrich potential B2B customer leads in the restaurant and hospitality industry.

The main goal is lead collection and contact enrichment for future outreach. The dashboard is now a lightweight manual fill tool for reviewing country-level lead gaps and exporting updated CSV files.

The current active focus is Australia first. The near-term workflow should prioritize Australia restaurant and hotel final customers, key-person contact discovery, and a repeatable process that can later become a local tool.

The CSV and dashboard now separate company-level contact channels from key contact person information so outreach readiness is easier to judge. The current dashboard should stay simple: country filter first, then manual filling of missing company contact, form, and key-person fields.

## Folder Structure

```text
project-folder/
  README.md
  data/
    leads.csv
    contacts.csv
    search_keywords.csv
    keyword_runs.csv
    outreach_log.csv
  dashboard/
    index.html
    app.js
    style.css
  docs/
    project-overview.md
    lead-collection-workflow.md
    lead-enrichment-workflow.md
    lead-table-fields.md
    dashboard-guide.md
    iteration-setup.md
    cli-operating-rules.md
    request-solution-log.md
    current-progress.md
  backup/
  exports/
  reports/
  skills/
```

## Specialized Documentation

- `docs/project-overview.md`: Business background, target customers, and MVP scope.
- `docs/lead-collection-workflow.md`: Lead discovery workflow and public-source rules.
- `docs/lead-enrichment-workflow.md`: Contact enrichment workflow and stopping rules.
- `docs/lead-table-fields.md`: `leads.csv` fields, statuses, scoring, and value rules.
- `docs/dashboard-guide.md`: Local country-based dashboard fill/export workflow.
- `docs/iteration-setup.md`: Backups, timestamps, update tags, and rollback.
- `docs/cli-operating-rules.md`: Which files Codex should read for each task type.
- `docs/request-solution-log.md`: Concise record of important user requests and CLI/Codex handling plans.
- `docs/current-progress.md`: Current project state, next work, and commands for resuming.

## Current Data Files

- `data/leads.csv`: Lead records and enrichment status.
- `data/contacts.csv`: Person-level contact records linked to `leads.csv` by `lead_id`.
- `data/search_keywords.csv`: Search keyword templates for lead discovery.
- `data/keyword_runs.csv`: Keyword run and batch tracking.
- `data/outreach_log.csv`: Future outreach conflict-prevention template. Do not fill it until outreach is actually planned or sent.

## Project Tools

- `dashboard/index.html`, `dashboard/app.js`, `dashboard/style.css`: Manual fill dashboard for reviewing country-level lead gaps, editing company/contact fields, and exporting canonical CSV files.
- `scripts/start_dashboard.ps1`: Starts the local dashboard server so the browser can read default CSV files.
- `scripts/build_au_review_queue.py`: Auxiliary historical Australia review queue; this segmentation path is paused unless explicitly requested.
- `scripts/build_form_kp_candidate_queue.py`: Builds the current Australia form/KP queue from existing CSV data and marks missing form, KP, or direct-contact gaps.
- `scripts/generate_kp_search_tasks.py`: Builds direct KP search-task queues from existing leads, prioritizing Australia restaurant/hotel final-customer candidates, known KP names, official-site queries, PDF/news queries, and LinkedIn manual-review entry points.
- `scripts/extract_public_contact_candidates.py`: Uses Scrapling static, dynamic, or stealth fetching to extract candidate emails, phones, contact/team links, LinkedIn URLs, and role snippets from queued official websites. Supports one-level same-domain link follow-up and outputs candidate evidence for CSV registration with confidence/status notes.
- `scripts/check_capability_inventory.py`: Checks whether the current computer has the project capabilities needed for the current workflow and suggests setup commands when something is missing.
- `requirements.txt`: Python package inventory for recreating the project-local extraction environment.
- Project-local `.venv`: Preferred Python environment for newly installed tools. Do not use global C: drive installs when the tool can run from the project directory.
- Project-local `.ms-playwright`: Preferred Playwright browser directory. Use `PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH` to keep browser binaries outside C: when possible.
- Project-local `.codex/tools/mcp-notifications`: Node/npm MCP server used for lightweight desktop notifications from Codex. `node_modules` is ignored; reinstall with `npm.cmd install --prefix .codex\tools\mcp-notifications @topvisor/mcp-notifications` if needed.
- Scrapling `Fetcher`: Static public-page extraction to reduce manual page reading and token usage. Installed in `.venv`.
- Scrapling `DynamicFetcher`: Browser extraction for pages that need JavaScript rendering. Uses project-local Playwright Chromium.
- Scrapling `StealthyFetcher`: Stealth browser extraction for approved high-capability runs with anti-ban controls. Uses project-local Playwright/Patchright dependencies.
- Playwright / Chromium: Browser runtime used by Scrapling dynamic and stealth fetchers. Installed under `.ms-playwright`.
- `data/search_keywords.csv` and `data/keyword_runs.csv`: Keyword and batch-control tracking for repeatable discovery tests.
- `reports/*.md` and `reports/*.csv`: Batch outputs, candidate queues, test results, and evidence summaries.
- `docs/request-solution-log.md`: Required task-end log for important requests, tool decisions, workflow changes, file-changing tasks, and future operating context.
- `.codex/config.toml` and `.codex/hooks/*.py`: Project-local Codex hooks for selected guardrails: destructive command blocking, request-log reminders, current-progress threshold warnings, script compile checks, capability inventory reminders, high-capability extraction scope warnings, and UTF-8 Markdown checks.

## Capability Setup

Use the project-local environment first:

```powershell
cd E:\AI\Codex\TestProject
python -m venv .venv
.\.venv\Scripts\python -m pip install -r requirements.txt
$env:PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH='E:\AI\Codex\TestProject\.ms-playwright'
.\.venv\Scripts\python -m playwright install chromium
.\.venv\Scripts\python scripts\check_capability_inventory.py
```

When moving to another computer, run the inventory check first. If a task fails repeatedly and the existing tools are not enough, evaluate adding a new tool, skill, library, or language and record it in this section plus `docs/request-solution-log.md`.

## Current Operating Priority

- Market: Australia first.
- Customer focus: restaurant and hotel final customers first.
- Contact focus: key-person email first, key-person phone second, company-level contact paths after that.
- Tool goal: turn the proven Australia search, scoring, KP enrichment, CSV update, and reporting workflow into a repeatable local tool.

## Resume This Project

From PowerShell:

```powershell
cd E:\AI\Codex\TestProject
git pull
codex "Read README.md, docs/current-progress.md, docs/request-solution-log.md, docs/cli-operating-rules.md, docs/lead-enrichment-workflow.md, and docs/lead-collection-workflow.md first. Then continue the current Ron Group Australia restaurant/hotel lead toolization work. You may iterate automatically inside the approved task scope, but do not start a new business collection or enrichment iteration beyond that scope without explicit approval."
```

## Local Dashboard

Start the dashboard through the local server so the browser can read the default CSV files:

```powershell
cd E:\AI\Codex\TestProject
.\scripts\start_dashboard.ps1
```

Then use `http://localhost:8765/dashboard/`. The dashboard supports manual filling of missing company contact, form, key-person, and all linked contact fields, then exports canonical `leads.csv` and `contacts.csv` files with editor name, timestamp, and change notes. It does not collect leads, enrich contacts automatically, send emails, log in to platforms, or call AI APIs.

<!-- CODEx_END: project_index -->

# AGENTS.md

## Project Purpose

This repository is the Ron Group local lead research MVP. The active workflow is Australia-first restaurant/hotel final-customer lead enrichment, with key-person contact discovery as the main bottleneck.

## Read First

At the start of a Codex session in this project folder, use this file as the entry rule and read the relevant active Markdown before acting.

For broad, unclear, configuration, tooling, or workflow tasks, read these files before acting:

- `README.md`
- `docs/cli-operating-rules.md`
- `docs/request-solution-log.md`
- `docs/codex-agent-usage.md`
- `docs/lead-enrichment-workflow.md`
- `docs/lead-collection-workflow.md`
- `docs/lead-table-fields.md`

For narrow tasks, read `docs/cli-operating-rules.md` first, then follow its task-specific context list. For Codex hook, agent, config, or startup errors, also read `docs/codex-agent-usage.md` before editing or troubleshooting.

Use `docs/current-progress.md` only for handoff/checkpoint context. Do not update it for routine or early small iterations.

## Operating Rules

- Prefer official company websites, official contact pages, official PDFs, and reviewable public evidence.
- Do not fabricate contacts, job titles, emails, phones, or LinkedIn URLs.
- Treat LinkedIn personal URLs as manual review entry points unless a person/company match is verified. Do not automate LinkedIn login, browsing, messaging, or export without explicit approval.
- Extracted company contact routes and KP candidates may be written to `data/leads.csv` or `data/contacts.csv` when a public source is recorded. Mark uncertain KP rows as Low/Medium confidence and pending verification; do not fabricate or mark them as verified direct contacts.
- Preserve row counts and CSV schemas unless the user explicitly approves a schema/data migration.
- Keep the dashboard simple. Do not add new segmentation or classification layers unless explicitly requested.
- Use project-local tooling first: `.venv`, `.ms-playwright`, `requirements.txt`, and `scripts/check_capability_inventory.py`.
- Keep important durable decisions in `docs/request-solution-log.md`. Include `Achievements` and `Issues / limits` lists for new task records.
- Default to writing the `docs/request-solution-log.md` entry in the same turn when a task changes files, rules, workflow, data standards, tools, or future operating context, unless the user explicitly forbids it or limits edits to files that exclude the log.
- Preserve user and project content formatting by default. Only add boundary labels, compact repeated structures, or strict machine-readable formats when the trigger rules in `docs/cli-operating-rules.md` are clearly met.
- Before modifying Codex hooks, confirm the current Codex-supported TOML structure. Do not copy Claude Code `[[hooks]] event = "..."` hook config directly into Codex; use Codex event-grouped hook tables instead.

## Commands

Capability check:

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python scripts\check_capability_inventory.py
```

Python compile check:

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python -m py_compile scripts\build_form_kp_candidate_queue.py scripts\extract_public_contact_candidates.py scripts\generate_kp_form_queries.py scripts\check_capability_inventory.py
```

Small public candidate extraction test:

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python scripts\extract_public_contact_candidates.py --limit 3 --follow-links 1 --delay 2 --output-prefix reports\test-candidates
```

Dashboard:

```powershell
.\scripts\start_dashboard.ps1
```

## Documentation Rules

- `docs/request-solution-log.md`: durable user requests, workflow/tool behavior changes, file-changing tasks, achievements, issues, follow-up.
- `docs/current-progress.md`: only account/session handoff, context-risk checkpoint, meaningful completed stage, major data/tool milestone, or resume-critical next plan.
- `docs/lead-enrichment-workflow.md`: contact/KP confidence, LinkedIn boundaries, extraction helper behavior.
- `docs/lead-collection-workflow.md`: source/tool access and collection scope.
- `docs/cli-operating-rules.md`: agent behavior, checkpoint rules, project-level boundaries.

## Safety Boundaries

- Ask before broad new collection/enrichment batches, schema changes, high-risk access modes, authenticated platform automation, paid-source extraction, CAPTCHA handling, or account-risky workflows.
- Do not run destructive commands such as recursive delete, `git reset --hard`, or forced checkout unless the user explicitly requested and approved the action.

4	*Updated: 2026-05-14 12:20*
5	
6	## Current State
7	
8	This repository is the Ron Group local lead research MVP.
9	
10	Active direction:
11	
12	- Market: Australia first.
13	- Customer focus: restaurant and hotel final customers first.
14	- Contact focus: key-person email first, key-person phone second, company-level routes after that.
15	- Tool goal: make the Australia search, scoring, KP enrichment, CSV update, and reporting workflow repeatable locally.
16	
17	Current data snapshot:
18	
19	- `data/leads.csv`: 195 rows, 53 fields.
20	- `data/contacts.csv`: 128 rows, 21 fields.
21	- `data/search_keywords.csv`: 38 keyword rows.
22	- `data/keyword_runs.csv`: 23 run rows.
23	- Australia leads: 80.
24	
25	Important current CSV registration rule:
26	
27	- Machine-generated public-source company routes and KP candidates may be written directly to `data/leads.csv` or `data/contacts.csv` when they include a reviewable source, confidence/status, and uncertainty notes.
28	- Human exclusion/review is required before actual outreach, not before candidate registration.
29	- Uncertain KP rows must stay Low/Medium confidence and pending verification.
30	- Do not save pure role snippets, fetch status rows, generic search-result URLs, or unsourced claims as contact records.
31	- LinkedIn/profile URLs are manual-review entry points unless person/company fit is verified; do not automate LinkedIn login, browsing, messaging, scraping, or export without separate explicit approval.
32	
33	## Latest Tavily / Scrapling Testing
34	
35	Tavily remains experimental. Do not promote it into active workflow docs until the user reviews and approves a stable operating method.
36	
37	Most relevant files:
38	
39	- 20-object fair validation:
40	  - `reports/tavily-validation-20260514/outputs/compact-review-report.md`
41	  - `reports/tavily-validation-20260514/outputs/metrics.csv`
42	- 50-object expanded validation:
43	  - `reports/tavily-validation-20260514-expanded50/outputs/compact-review-report.md`
44	  - `reports/tavily-validation-20260514-expanded50/outputs/metrics.csv`
45	  - `reports/tavily-validation-20260514-expanded50/outputs/comparison-matrix.csv`
46	- Prior decision brief:
47	  - `reports/tavily-experiment-decision-brief-20260513.md`
48	- Current form/Scrapling baseline:
49	  - `reports/au-form-kp-candidate-extraction-test-complete-20260512.md`
50	  - `reports/au-public-contact-candidates-20260513-form-more-static12.md`
51	
52	50-object expanded validation summary:
53	
54	- Validation set: 25 likely valid targets, 15 borderline/review objects, 10 noisy/non-target domains.
55	- `A_basic_control`: official precision 38.0%, noise 80.0%, contact route 100.0%, KP 100.0%, 10 credits.
56	- `B_filtered_search`: official precision 21.5%, noise 20.0%, contact route 91.3%, KP 78.3%, 47 credits.
57	- `C_advanced_exact_kp`: official precision 20.6%, noise 23.4%, contact route 52.2%, KP 87.0%, 98 credits.
58	- `D_map_official_pages`: official precision 99.0%, noise 0.0%, contact route 88.9%, KP 55.6%, 10 credits.
59	- `S_scrapling_form_baseline`: official precision 99.5%, noise 0.0%, contact route 84.4%, KP 81.2%, measured from existing reports rather than a new timed run.
60	- Four Tavily requests failed with SSL EOF/handshake timeout errors; the run completed and recorded them.
61	
62	Candidate combined workflow from the latest report:
63	
64	1. Start from a known official domain when available; otherwise use `B_filtered_search` to discover candidate domains and reject obvious noisy domains.
65	2. Run `D_map_official_pages` on official domains to discover compact contact/about/team/news URLs before fetching page text.
66	3. Pass useful official URLs to Scrapling follow-links to extract visible forms, emails, phones, and page-level KP hints.
67	4. Run `C_advanced_exact_kp` only for leads still missing named KP evidence, using cleaned company names and official-domain context.
68	5. Keep contact registration separate from final outreach readiness: register traceable low/medium-confidence candidates, then exclude or upgrade before outreach.
69	
70	## Key Files To Read
71	
72	For the next session, read in this order:
73	
74	1. `AGENTS.md`
75	2. `README.md`
76	3. `docs/current-progress.md`
77	4. `docs/request-solution-log.md`
78	5. `docs/cli-operating-rules.md`
79	6. `docs/lead-enrichment-workflow.md`
80	7. `docs/lead-table-fields.md`
81	8. `reports/tavily-validation-20260514-expanded50/outputs/compact-review-report.md`
82	9. `reports/tavily-validation-20260514-expanded50/outputs/metrics.csv`
83	10. `reports/au-form-kp-candidate-extraction-test-complete-20260512.md`
84	11. `reports/au-public-contact-candidates-20260513-form-more-static12.md`
85	
86	For Codex hook/config/startup issues, also read:
87	
88	- `docs/codex-agent-usage.md`
89	
90	## Recommended Next Judgment Task
91	
92	The next session should not immediately run a larger Tavily batch.
93	
94	Recommended task:
95	
96	- Review the 50-object expanded validation and current Scrapling/form baseline.
97	- Decide whether the proposed B + D_map + Scrapling + selective C workflow is strong enough to become the next experimental operating method.
98	- If yes, design a small production-style reducer/test that registers traceable candidates under the current CSV rule, without treating them as outreach-verified.
99	- If no, identify which metric is still too weak: domain precision, KP evidence quality, review rows per valid lead, runtime, credits, or registration confidence.
100	
101	Guardrails for next execution:
102	
103	- Do not write broad source CSV changes without a scoped plan.
104	- Do not start 100+ Tavily runs unless explicitly approved.
105	- Do not update active workflow docs with Tavily as standard until the user approves the operating method.
106	- Keep Tavily/Scrapling experimental outputs in `reports/` and durable decisions in `docs/request-solution-log.md`.
107	
108	## Useful Commands
109	
110	Resume safely:
111	
112	```powershell
113	cd E:\AI\Codex\TestProject
114	git status --short
115	.\.venv\Scripts\python scripts\check_capability_inventory.py
116	.\.venv\Scripts\python -m py_compile scripts\build_form_kp_candidate_queue.py scripts\extract_public_contact_candidates.py scripts\generate_kp_search_tasks.py scripts\run_tavily_search_tasks.py scripts\run_tavily_discovery_experiment.py scripts\check_capability_inventory.py
117	```

119	Open the latest Tavily report:
120

122	Get-Content -Raw reports\tavily-validation-20260514-expanded50\outputs\compact-review-report.md
123	```
124	
125	Run the dashboard:
